name: Barry Ulanov
abstract: Baruch "Barry" Ulanov (April 10, 1918 – April 30, 2000) was an American writer, perhaps best known as a jazz critic.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I found out years later – lambasted by Barry Ulanov in Metronome when it came out in 1942. That review is the first shot fired in what became this protracted journalist, critical warfare between what eventually became moldy figs and progressivists.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only responds to the interviewer's references to Barry Ulanov's books; there is no evidence of a personal relationship, meeting, or collaboration between the interviewee (Juan Tizol) and Barry Ulanov.

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee responds to questions about statements made by Barry Ulanov in his Duke Ellington book, but there is no evidence of a personal relationship between Lawrence Brown and Barry Ulanov.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Barry Ulanov in the context of a review, but the interviewee (Dave Brubeck) does not respond or provide any personal interaction details.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: He took me down to Barry Ulanov's house. He spoke to him about me... So Barry said, 'I'll get one of the greatest priest's that's ever lived.'

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Ramey
about_quotes.evidence: So Barry Ulanov and several of them come over there almost threatening me, you know, said, 'Look, you got to go back with McShann.'
